title: Estepona Dolphin Watching Boat Rental: Costs and Tips for 2026
html:
Dolphin watching boat rental in Estepona offers a chance to spot common dolphins and pilot whales in the Bay of Gibraltar. Depart from Puerto Deportivo de Estepona with a private motorboat from €350 for a half-day or a shared tour from €69 per person on BoatHire24, where you can compare verified boat listings for the best options.

Where to set off in Estepona

Estepona’s main departure point is Puerto Deportivo de Estepona, a modern marina with full facilities and easy access to the Strait of Gibraltar. Other launch spots include Playa del Cristo, a sheltered bay popular for small boat launches, and the nearby Puerto de la Duquesa marina, 12 nautical miles east. For dolphin watching, most charters head to the waters off Punta Doncella or towards the Bay of Algeciras, where the current-rich Strait attracts feeding dolphins. The coastal town of San Roque also offers alternative boat access for extended trips. You can compare verified boat listings in a few minutes.

Which boat to rent

Choose a boat based on group size and the experience you want. Here are four popular options. One option worth a look is this motor yacht.

  1. Rigid inflatable boat (RIB), fast and agile for chasing dolphin pods, ideal for 6 to 8 guests, from €350 half-day.
  2. Motor yacht, comfortable with a saloon and sun deck, perfect for up to 12 guests, from €800 half-day; on BoatHire24 you can find this motor yacht such as the Princess F70 listing.
  3. Sailing catamaran, stable and spacious, suitable for up to 18 guests on shared tours, from €69 per person.
  4. Luxury flybridge, premium amenities for up to 10 guests, from €1,500 full-day; models like the Azimut 60 or Sunseeker Predator are common.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Estepona?

Prices range from €69 per person for a shared tour to €1,500 per day for a crewed yacht. The exact rate depends on boat type, duration, and the season. Below is a typical price band breakdown. If you own a boat, you can list a boat for free and earn from spare days.

  • Shared dolphin-watching tour: €69 to €89 per person, 2 hours.
  • Small motorboat half-day (no crew): €350 to €500 for up to 8 guests.
  • Catamaran full-day (with skipper): €1,200 to €1,800 for up to 18 guests.
  • Luxury motor yacht full-day (crewed): €1,500 to €3,000 for up to 12 guests.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Estepona?

In Spain you need a recreational boating licence (PER or PNB) to skipper a boat over 6 metres length. For smaller vessels under 6 metres, no licence is required but operators may still require a brief checkout. The BoatHire24 operator will confirm exact requirements when you book. Always verify before you set off.

In summer, the Levante wind can kick up choppy seas in the afternoon, so book an early morning trip for the calmest dolphin-viewing conditions.

At a glance

  • Price band: €69 per person to €3,000 per day
  • Peak months: June through September
  • Typical trip length: 2 to 8 hours
  • Guest capacity: 6 to 18 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Estepona

What is the best time of year for dolphin watching in Estepona?

The best season runs from April to October, with peak sightings in June and July when the Strait of Gibraltar is richest in marine life.

Can I rent a boat in Estepona without a skipper?

Yes, you can rent a small motorboat without a skipper if you hold the appropriate Spanish licence or an International Certificate of Competence, but most new renters prefer a charter with a skipper for dolphin watching.

How long does a dolphin watching trip from Estepona last?

Most trips last between 2 and 4 hours, covering outbound travel of 8 to 12 nautical miles to the feeding grounds near the Strait of Gibraltar.

Are there any age or safety restrictions for children?

Children are welcome on most boats, but life jackets must be worn at all times and minimum ages may apply on RIBs; check with the operator before booking.

Whether you are a first-time visitor or a seasoned boater, renting a boat in Estepona through BoatHire24 gives you access to verified boats and transparent pricing. If you own a boat in the area, you can list a boat for free on the platform and start earning income from the growing demand for dolphin watching charters in 2026.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Estepona and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
